coverage_scope: Simplified QBI deduction for Schedule C and K-1 inputs
summary: Now includes Schedule C net profit in the business-income, Schedule SE, QBI trigger, and Form 8995 simplified computation path, and it sources wage/UBIA inputs from Schedule C business metadata and K-1 section 199A metadata instead of hardcoding zeros.
remaining_gap: Still lacks REIT/PTP components, prior-year QBI loss carryovers, rental QBI qualification logic, SSTB-specific handling, and full IRS attachment behavior for overflow businesses.

coverage_scope: Detailed QBI computation with wage/property limitation
summary: Now keeps per-business wage and UBIA limitation math separated across lines A/B/C, uses real wage/UBIA sourcing, and aggregates detailed QBI deductions across more than three business entries for calculation parity even when the PDF only exposes three slots.
remaining_gap: Still lacks patron reductions, REIT/PTP, DPAD, SSTB/aggregation elections, and true attachment generation for businesses beyond the paper-form slots.

coverage_scope: Thresholds, SSTB phaseout, wage/property limitation, REIT/PTP component
summary: Stronger than UsTaxes on thresholding, SSTB applicable percentage, wage/property limitation, and REIT/PTP handling in the central QBI engine.
remaining_gap: Form 8995-A resource is still effectively single-business oriented and does not model the full IRS attachment/aggregation surface.

coverage_scope: Part I rental property income/expense matrix plus K-1 page 2 columns
scenario_refs: UsTaxes scenario18.test.ts | UsTaxes scenarioNR2.test.ts | UsTaxes scenario3.test.ts
summary: Now implements rental receipts, major expense rows, vacation-home use test, 1099-MISC royalty income, optional royalty expenses, page 2 estate/trust REMIC and farm-rental branches, and passive loss limitation through Form 8582 without recursing on royalty-only returns.
remaining_gap: Modified AGI in F8582 is still simplified, richer K-1 page 2 rental/royalty breakdowns remain under-modeled, and PDF support is still capped to 3 properties and 4 K-1s.

coverage_scope: Single-property rental/royalty facts with passive-loss allowance derivation
summary: Stronger than UsTaxes on derived passive-loss allowance mechanics and explicit rental fact graph calculations.
remaining_gap: Resource is mostly single-property oriented and does not expose the full Schedule E page 2 K-1 matrix or multi-property column behavior expected on the paper form.

coverage_scope: Nonresident wrapper for ECI, FDAP, treaty, deductions, and payment inputs
scenario_refs: UsTaxes scenarioNR1.test.ts | UsTaxes scenarioNR2.test.ts | UsTaxes scenarioNR3.test.ts | UsTaxes scenarioNR4.test.ts | UsTaxes scenarioNR12.test.ts | f1040nrParity.test.ts
summary: Now computes taxable ECI, tax on ECI using the 2025 ordinary tax table, total tax, and refund/balance-due outputs instead of only exposing raw inputs.
remaining_gap: Still far from full line-by-line 1040-NR parity: no explicit Schedule OI item mapping, no schedule NEC detail table, treaty modeling is simplified, and ATS scenario output coupling is still loose.

coverage_scope: Fact graph for nonresident flag, ECI, FDAP, treaty, deductions, and total tax
summary: Now derives taxOnECI from the published 2025 bracket schedule instead of leaving it writable, and the ATS converter now emits explicit filing-status booleans plus core nonresident facts for country, ECI, FDAP, and itemized deduction backfills.
remaining_gap: Still simplified versus the actual 1040-NR package: Schedule OI/NEC specifics are under-modeled, treaty detail remains shallow, and several ATS scenario branches still depend on fixture backfills more than fully explicit form facts.

coverage_scope: Worker-backed local auth, CRUD, review, submission, rejection, state-transfer, print-mail
scenario_refs: worker runtime e2e | local TaxFlow e2e
summary: Local end-to-end worker-backed flow is now green and can be used to validate future form-parity fixes through the TaxFlow surface.
remaining_gap: Harness validates lifecycle orchestration, but deeper form-output parity still depends on the underlying tax-engine form implementations listed above.
